title: agent.gate
bullet_1: Governance policy enforcement and auditability for AI-assisted software delivery

title: Executive summary (30 seconds)
bullet_1: Policy-as-code deterministic checks and tamper-evident audit trails in the same workflows as coding agents and developers
bullet_2: Connects MCP OPA-style decisions Neon/Postgres persistence and an admin dashboard for policy lifecycle and signal intelligence
bullet_3: Positioning: standalone governance layer—not a SonarQube narrative and not tied to Domino REST

title: Problem / market gap
bullet_1: Agent-generated code scales faster than governance: reviews guardrails and evidence often lag
bullet_2: Tooling fragmentation: LLM tools IDEs CI and policy engines rarely share a common contract for what was checked and why
bullet_3: Weak audit narrative for risk teams: model said it is fine is not the same as signed chained queryable decision logs
bullet_4: Human rejection signals are unstructured: PR feedback rarely flows back into automated rules or coverage metrics

title: What agent.gate is
bullet_1: Governance engine exposing MCP tools to audit code log transactions and produce audit artifacts on demand
bullet_2: Policy bridge to OPA for structured allow/deny evaluation (agent.gate decision model)
bullet_3: Persistence layer: Postgres for decision logs rules_library and pr_signals analytics
bullet_4: Admin dashboard: policy IDE natural-language rule assistance governance console Signal Intelligence

title: Core architecture
bullet_1: MCP server (streamable-http / stdio): integration surface for coding agents and automation
bullet_2: Policy evaluation: OPA-backed decisions with pragmatic fallbacks when services are unavailable
bullet_3: Data plane: Postgres for decision logs rules_library pr_signals rejection analytics
bullet_4: Optional: semantic search over governance standards to ground policy suggestions
bullet_5: Admin UI: Vite/React console for visibility and operations

title: Key features — developer & agent experience
bullet_1: audit_vibe: profile-aware checks with HTML certificates and configurable outputs
bullet_2: Profiles and rule packs: baseline and industry-style profiles; shared rules_library with tenant industry compliance tags domain filters
bullet_3: list_vibe_rules / list_vibe_profiles: discoverability for agents and humans
bullet_4: Natural-language to structured rule assistance with machine-testable patterns (e.g. regex test hooks in Rego workflows)

title: Key features — GRC-grade auditability
bullet_1: log_vibe_transaction: evaluate proposed changes with intent context and persist structured outcomes
bullet_2: Integrity: hash-chained logs and RSA signing/verification for tamper detection (verify_audit_integrity)
bullet_3: generate_audit_report: Software Bill of Intent style reporting over a time window
bullet_4: log_audit_trail: structured provenance suitable for downstream review

title: Key features — Signal Intelligence
bullet_1: Ingest PR rejection patterns into pr_signals to answer where are we blind
bullet_2: Dashboard widgets: coverage gaps priority signals automation potential; velocity-style narratives
bullet_3: Signal Agent: conversational assistant over signals and active policy context (summaries filters drafting staging-style workflows)

title: Value proposition
bullet_1: Speed with control: policy enforcement and evidence at AI coding throughput
bullet_2: A single integration contract (MCP) across tools instead of bespoke per-IDE plugins
bullet_3: Defensible compliance posture: decisions as data—queryable attributable verifiable
bullet_4: Closed-loop governance: human review signals operationalized into coverage and candidate policies

title: Customer value
bullet_1: Risk reduction: fewer unknown-unknowns in agent-generated diffs; clearer deny reasons and repeatable checks
bullet_2: Operational efficiency: less manual re-review of the same failure classes; faster rejection-to-rule convergence
bullet_3: Multitenancy readiness: tenant-scoped patterns for decisions signals and rules activation
bullet_4: Executive-friendly reporting: certificates and summaries in governance language

title: Differentiation
bullet_1: Not positioned as SonarQube but LLM: the story is agent-era governance infrastructure—contracts policy evidence feedback loops
bullet_2: Explicit non-goal: no dependency on Domino REST API
bullet_3: Composable: MCP + OPA + Postgres + optional embeddings—incremental pilot to expand

title: Typical buyers / users
bullet_1: Platform engineering / developer productivity: standardize guardrails for agents
bullet_2: Security and GRC: auditability integrity reporting
bullet_3: App teams shipping fast with Copilot-like workflows: pragmatic guardrails without blocking experimentation

title: Implementation footprint
bullet_1: Deployable: MCP (e.g. Vercel) dashboard/API routes Neon Postgres optional OPA optional vector search
bullet_2: Telemetry naming discipline: agent.gate.* spans and agent.gate.issues.* style metrics

title: Roadmap / maturity notes
bullet_1: Strong: MCP surface OPA patterns persistence + integrity primitives dashboard PR signal ingestion/analytics UI
bullet_2: Productization opportunities: durable server workflows for approve-and-deploy style actions; clustering to master policy automation; deeper CI gates and policy promotion beyond UI mocks where applicable

title: Closing — one-liner
bullet_1: agent.gate = governance that keeps pace with agents
bullet_2: One-liner: Policy-backed checks signed audit trails and rejection intelligence—wired directly into how software is generated today
